free_text: Light charged particle emission in fast neutron
(25 - 65 MeV) induced reactions on Bi-209
Raeymackers, Benck, Slypen, Meulders
Nica, Corcalciuc
Koning, Nuclear Research and Consultancy
Group NRG, Petten, Netherlands
Main reference,Cross sections
are given
Data tables are given for
Bi-209 and U-nat. Experimental details.
In French
Final data for Bi-209, U-nat,
Co-59, and Fe-nat
Analysis details
- thick target
correction procedure by Monte Carlo simulation program
Description of the
CYCLONE facility
Fast neutron facility cyclotron
'CYCLONE'
65 MeV proton beam strikes 3 mm thick
natural lithium target. Proton current is 12 uA
producing aprx. 1.0E+5 neutrons/cm2/sec at the
location of the target
Full width at half maximum is aprx. 2 MeV containing
90% of neutrons/MeV plus a flat continuum of
low-energy neutrons.
For energy calibration the proton and deuteron
recoiling from respectively a polypropylene (1 mm
thick) and deuterated polypropylene target (0.6 mm
thick) were used.
Four charged particles detector
consisting of six telescopes were used simultaneously,
each of them consists of
1.  'Delta-E' Detector NE102 plastic scintillator,
0.1 mm thick, 4 cm diameter, and
2.   Cs-I(Tl) 'E' detector, crystal 22 mm thick,
38.1 mm diameter
The time-of-flight method was deduced from the
time difference between the capacitive beam pickoff
located upstream from the neutron-producing target,
and the delta-E detector. Only those events of
charged particles detection  were registered which
are associated with neutrons in the main neutron
peak.
Cross Sections were Measured
at degrees between 20 and 70 degree with step 10 deg
in each of the telescopes
Square metallic bismuth 6*6 cm2, 0.315 mm thick.
Purity 99.99%, density 9.831 gram/cm3
The solid angle and thick target corrections were
made with the help of Monte Carlo simulation program
Multistep direct models of Feshbach-Kerman-
Koonin, Distorted Wave Born Approximation and
Statistical Description of excited states
Comparison with theoretical calculations based
on TALYS and GNASH.
Total error includes ERR-SYS and DATA-ERR
uncertainty of the cross section
absolute scale due to:
uncertainty in the measured reference (n,p)
cross sections (5%),
uncertainty in the beam monitoring (2%),
uncertainty in the statistics in the
H(n,p) recoil proton peak (2-5%),
uncertainty in solid angle corrections (1%),
uncertainty in number of target nuclei, etc.
The overall relative uncertainties of
the experimental points in the spectra are about 6.%,
9.%, 20.% and 25% for respectively p, d, t and alpha at
62.7 MeV. They are mainly given by the statistics in
the spectra. At lower ejectile energies, the thick
target corrections contribute with supplementary
uncertainties. For all the other incident neutron
energies (continuum), these values are between 2 and 3
times higher as a consequence of the lower incident
neutron flux. (info from thesis)
* EN-RSL -half width of the incident neutron energy bin
Thesis of Raeyemackers; the same in data set
SM
Author name corrected - Raeyemackers,
SAMPLE information changed  - SM
SD:Part of refs moved to REL-REF (used only
for experim.details description). In Subents 027-033,
037,041,045 second term of REACTION code deleted (3He
production negligible - remark from S.Benck).
Calculated data lines (marked by FLAG) were moved to
separated REACTION (multiple reaction formalism used)
in Subents 002 - 037. ERR-ANALYS update.
DATA-ERR -> ERR-T in Subents 002-045. All values=0.
without error are deleted following Tbl.2 of
AND,87,231,2004 and confirmation from Dr.S.Benck.
SD: ERR-ANALYS updated. DATA-ERR -> ERR-6.

following Tbl.3 of AND,87,231,2004. DATA-ERR -> ERR-T.
